title: Anis Abdulle - Khoury College of Computer Sciences
text:
Anis Abdulle - Khoury College of Computer Sciences
All People
Anis Abdulle
(he/him/his)
Associate Co-op Coordinator
Education
Education
MEd in Higher Education Administration, Northeastern University

Biography
Biography
Anis Abdulle is an associate co-op coordinator at the Khoury College of Computer Sciences at Northeastern University. He received his master’s in higher education administration and bachelor’s in psychology from Northeastern. Prior to working with the Khoury College co-op team, he worked at Northeastern’s D’Amore-McKim School of Business co-op office as an academic assistant and then as a data administrator.
Abdulle’s role at Khoury College entails supporting students both in their preparation to go out on co-op and during their co-op experience. This includes resume and interview support to best position students to land co-op opportunities at one of Northeastern’s employer partners. Additionally, he reviews and comments on students’ reflections on guided inquiries based on their co-op experiences. He also engages in job development research, events, and operations support for Khoury College co-op programs.
Abdulle is a long-time admirer of Northeastern’s co-op program, which gives students firsthand experiences in their various fields of study. Having seen the benefits of the co-op program and model both as a student and staff at Northeastern, he enjoys being able to continue his engagement with the co-op program at Khoury College and support students to fulfill their educational and professional aspirations.
Abdulle was born and raised in Somalia. He spent some of his most defining childhood years in Ethiopia and Kenya before moving to the U.S. in 2006.
